column2: here are the dominant changes that occurred in the Supaul district over the 9-year period. Since Supaul is heavily influenced by the volatile Kosi River

column1: 1. Massive Shift from Crops to Trees (The Dominant Transition)
Insight: A staggering 728.53 sq km of land transitioned from Crops to Trees. In fact
column2: more cropland was converted to trees than remained stable (615.92 sq km).
this points to a massive surge in agroforestry

column1: 2. High Water Volatility (The Kosi River Effect)
Insight: While 54.54 sq km of water remained stable
column2: the district lost roughly 36.4 sq km of water bodies (mostly to trees and crops) while simultaneously gaining 35.3 sq km of new water from flooded agricultural land (27.61 sq km) and forests.

column1: Interpretation: This is a classic signature of the Kosi River floodplain. The river frequently changes its course
column2: depositing silt that eventually becomes farmable or vegetated land
column3: while simultaneously carving out new channels and flooding previously dry agricultural land.
